claim.statement: Both W(3,3) <= 27 and R(3,4) <= 9 hold: a conjoined certified-impossibility bundle over two independent combinatorial families.
claim.negation_of: a counterexample to either W(3,3) <= 27 or R(3,4) <= 9

rung.name: verified-checker-program
trusted_base: the union of both sub-barriers' bases (both R2 lratcheck)
min-trust rung = max(R2, R2, step R0) = R2

step.description: conjunction: both impossibilities hold independently; the AND-join is kernel-trivial (R0)

provenance.pipeline: recursively re-checks both sub-barriers, then asserts the declared rung equals the weakest link

one_directional: CERTIFIED only if BOTH sub-barriers re-certify AND the declared rung equals the min-trust (weakest) rung. A failing/deferred sub-barrier, or a declared rung stronger than the weakest link, fails closed -- you cannot launder a composite up to a rung none of its parts earned.
